mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals strengthening aggregate demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in payroll growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating federal funds rate hikes compress equity valuation multiples by raising the discount rate applied to future cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rate acceleration signal, or forward returns show no statistical difference from untriggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and residential construction financing indicates robust consumer confidence and credit availability, jointly boosting equity valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns above random chance across three consecutive rolling quarters, or individual series outperform the combined signal in predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production expansion reflects rising capacity utilization and operational leverage, which directly supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth decouples from equity returns, producing negative or neutral forward returns in > 50% of observation windows, or leading indicators turn negative while SPX continues rising.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP growth below 1.5% annualized signals an economic slowdown that erodes corporate revenue expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate falls below 1.5% annualized
failure_condition: Equity markets rally or remain flat despite confirmed GDP contraction below the threshold, or forward returns show no significant negative skew relative to baseline periods.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month increases in the consumer price index compress real yields and force central bank policy normalization, which systematically reduces equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: CPI increases fail to correlate with equity drawdowns, or forward returns remain positive during sustained inflationary periods, breaking the discount rate transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and declining unemployment rate confirm labor market normalization, reducing wage-price spiral risks while supporting consumer spending, which jointly drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces a negative or zero information coefficient over any 12-month rolling window, or unemployment declines without corresponding payroll gains fail to move markets.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts exceeding 5% over six months signals weakening consumer credit conditions and construction sector distress, which transmits to broader equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Housing starts decline while equity markets rally, or forward returns show no significant negative relationship to the housing contraction signal across multiple business cycles.
